name: facade
summary: Static-style accessor that proxies a DI-bound interface from non-injectable call sites.
description: Creates a facade class that proxies an interface
problem: You have an interface bound in the container, but some call sites (legacy templates, third-party hooks, global helper functions) can't receive constructor injection. You want a clean, static-feeling way to call into that interface without breaking encapsulation.
appliesWhen: An interface needs to be reachable from non-injectable contexts
You're integrating with a framework that calls into your code without DI (WordPress hooks, view templates, some legacy bridges)
You want explicit, named access points instead of service-locator style lookups
The call surface is small enough that a facade is cleaner than refactoring the call sites
avoidWhen: The call site can take constructor injection — just inject the interface directly
You're tempted to use a facade because DI feels like too much setup — that's a code smell
The proxy would expose every method on the interface — that's a sign DI is the right answer
examples: Add a Transactions facade for the payment service
Create a static-style accessor for the Mailer interface
I need to call the cache from a WordPress hook without injecting it
tradeoffs: Adds a layer of indirection. Convenient for awkward call sites, easy to overuse. Prefer constructor injection where possible.
outputs: A {name} facade class extending Facade and proxying the named interface
An abstractInstance() method returning the interface FQCN
A registration in the initializer's getFacades() list
postApply: Add typed static helper methods for the most-used interface methods, if you want IDE help
Confirm the facade resolves at runtime by calling it from the intended consumer

name.aiHint: PascalCase, plural or domain-flavored. Describes what the facade exposes (Transactions, Mailer, Cache).

interface.description: FQCN of the interface to proxy
interface.aiHint: Fully qualified interface name. The interface must already be DI-bound somewhere.

initializer.description: FQCN of the initializer to register in
initializer.aiHint: Pick the initializer that owns the underlying interface binding.
